informal_statement.text: Sort a mutable slice of integers in nondecreasing order using insertion sort. The function mutates its input in place and returns nothing.
required_properties: the resulting slice is sorted in nondecreasing order
the resulting slice is a permutation of the original slice
the function terminates on every input

semantic_units.description: Sortedness: the output list is sorted in nondecreasing order.
Permutation: the output is a permutation of the input (every element preserved, counts preserved).
common_failure_modes: asserting set equality without counting duplicates

semantic_units.description: Stability is NOT required and should not be asserted.

reference_obligations.nl_gloss: The output is sorted in nondecreasing order.
The output is a permutation of the input.

generated_obligations.nl_gloss: No explicit preconditions are stated in the provided Rust reference (the placeholder {{rust_reference}} contains no visible assertions/comments here). If the actual code includes preconditions (e.g., bounds on indices, non-emptiness, sortedness), they should be captured as separate obligations.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (input : α), let out := impl input; spec input out
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: State the core functional specification: for every input, the implementation’s output equals the mathematically defined result of the classical algorithm task described by the reference implementation.
Prove the implementation terminates (e.g., loop bounds decrease or recursion is well-founded), matching the control structure in the Rust code.
Prove that all array/vector indexing operations in the implementation are within bounds for all inputs satisfying the preconditions.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (input : α), invariant holds at loop/recursion start for input
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: For each loop/recursive function, provide the invariant and prove it holds initially.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (input : α), ∀ state, invariant state → invariant (step state)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: For each loop/recursive function, prove the invariant is preserved by each iteration/recursive step.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (input : α), invariant at exit → postcondition input (output)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: At loop/recursion exit, use the invariant (plus the negation of the loop condition) to derive the required postcondition.

generated_obligations.lean_statement: ∀ (input : α), edge_case_conditions input → spec input (impl input)
generated_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove correctness for boundary/edge cases explicitly handled by the code (e.g., empty input, singleton input, special-case branches).
